text_blocks.text: Red is the traditional color of
warning and danger. In the Middle Ages, a
red flag announced that the defenders of
a town or castle would fight to defend it,
and a red flag hoisted by a warship
meant they would show no mercy to their
enemy. In Britain, in the early days of
motoring, motor cars had to follow a man
with a red flag who would warn
on Highways Act 1896 abolished this law. In
automobile races, the red flag is raised if
there is danger to the drivers. In
international football, a player who has made
a serious violation of the rules is shown a
red penalty card and ejected from the game.
